title: pedicularis resupinata subsp. crassicaulis
intro: Pedicularis resupinata subsp. crassicaulis, also known as the Bear's Ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bumblebee flower genus Pedicularis. It is a perennial herb native to North America, and its medicinal properties lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health. The plant has been traditionally used to treat coughs, colds, and bronchitis due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, promoting overall respiratory well-being. Pedicularis resupinata subsp. crassicaulis can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies.
